entry.title: Preventive Support for SAP’s New Era of Customer Care
entry.content:
A few years ago, mobile phones did not have functions that nowadays we consider part of our routines such as video chat and music streaming. Today’s smartphones, moreover, have evolved to include integration of functions like camera, mobile payment and various apps. Similarly, customer care approaches have developed along with cellphones. Historically, the inability of businesses to afford real-time analysis meant that customer care was mostly reactive, dealing with issues as they arose.

In an era of real-time data, this backward-looking strategy presents a challenge companies need to overcome to understand customer behavior and boost business performance. Organizations now aim to retrieve information from past, present and future to gain a comprehensive view of their processes to forecast events and identify anomalies. Consequently, the outcomes expected will be the result of anticipated preparation and planning.

At SAP, the vast repository of historical issues, combined with extensive product knowledge and expertise, forms a rich foundation for preventive support. Some of the best practices to implement successful preventative customer care involve:

  • Using robust automation systems and AI models to process issue signals quickly.
  • Providing self-service opportunities through knowledge bases and chatbots.
  • Offering proactive support through notifications and updates.

AI and Machine Learning, alongside various data points and automation, enable us to identify patterns and issues, and to implement automated mitigation actions to prevent customers' business impact at early stages.

Furthermore, the potential of insights from critical event data as a strategic asset has expanded its use beyond traditional areas like marketing and sales. Now, departments such as customer service, finance, and operations management are also adopting this strategy. To assess the effectiveness of our customer care efforts, SAP employs several key metrics, including:

  • Customer Satisfaction Score (CSAT) and Net Promoter Score (NPS).
  • Customer Effort Score (CES) to rate how effortlessly customers were able to receive support.
  • Reduction in support calls or inquiries.
  • Production uptime and availability.

In this way, we monitor our progress while refining algorithms based on real-world feedback and performance metrics. For our customers, preventive support means an increase in reliability of their system that will lead to customer satisfaction. Additionally, they adapt to changing scenarios, therefore decision-making process will be based on real-time information.

Having successfully exercised this approach to support SAP Commerce Cloud customers during 2024 Black Friday and Cyber Monday sales events, we are at the moment expanding this model across other SAP products.

Just as smartphones have become essential through the integration of diverse functionalities, our preventive support approach seeks to be crucial in proactive customer care. As we continue to refine our AI models and expand our prognostic capabilities, we're committed to staying at the forefront of innovative solutions that keep businesses running smoothly.

At SAP Sapphire 2025, which will take place in Orlando and Madrid, we’ll hold sessions with experts to show how we support customers to better predict issues.

Check out this SAP News Center article to learn how proactive customer care can drive success during peak e-commerce sales periods.

A special thanks to SAP experts Bharath Ramamurthy, Vice President of Strategic Initiatives, Customer Support, and Martina Kiely, Business Lead of Technology & Transformation Office, Customer Support Experience, for their contributions to this article.

entry.title: Get the Best of your SAP IBP solution with the help of Continuous Quality Checks
entry.content:
Embarking on a project can be both exciting and daunting. One of the key aspects of a successful implementation is ensuring the quality and stability of your solution. SAP Enterprise Support offers continuous quality checks to support this goal. 

What are Continuous Quality Checks?

Continuous quality checks (CQCs) are remote services embedded in your SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain (SAP IBP) subscription. They are offered as part of your SAP Enterprise Support cloud edition maintenance contract at no extra cost.

The scope of the CQCs includes analyzing your project, system and/or specific situations to help reduce system risks and outline optimization potential. You will receive a report that includes an executive summary with findings and guidance for improvement.

Continuous Quality Checks for SAP IBP

Here is a list of continuous quality checks (CQCs) available to support your SAP IBP solution, tailored to your specific implementation phase and needs:

ServiceBenefitWhen to request
CQC for Transformation Assessment for SAP IBP - PREPAREA quick review of key decisions and knowledge points to ensure that your project is on the right track during its initial phases.(Highly recommended)
Prepare phase
(Pilot) CQC for Transformation Assessment for SAP IBP – Tactical Supply PlanningIdentifies incompatibilities between the Time Series algorithm and SAP IBP features and provides insights into the potential impact of implementing these features.(Highly recommended)
Prepare phase - particularly when planning to use the Time Series algorithm
CQC Deployment ReadinessProvides a review of key settings in your SAP IBP solution, including global configuration parameters and model configuration, to align with SAP best practices and support effective system setup.

Proactive. Performs a health check to verify the system configuration of SAP IBP production system.		(Highly recommended)
Realize phase - Two months prior to go live

(Proactive) Run phase
CQC Going Live SupportA post go-live analysis of system resource consumption.(Proactive)
Run phase - one month after go-live
CQC Business Process Performance Optimization (CQC BPPO)An analysis of selected performance analysis of your business-critical process steps for optimal performance improvement.(Proactive)
Realize/Deploy phase
Run phase

 

Additional Resource and Support

This blog post* offers a detailed information regarding CQC service for Transformation Assessment – PREPARE.

For information about CQC services for supply chain management, including how to request them, visit the Continuous Quality Checks* section of our Supply Chain Management Solutions value map*.

*To access the link and for ongoing support and actionable guidance tailored to your unique SAP journey, we invite you to join and explore SAP Enterprise Support Value Maps. 

Enhance Your SAP Experience with Value Maps 

Beyond CQC services, SAP Enterprise Support offers additional resources to help you drive innovation. SAP Enterprise Support value maps provide expert-led resources, tailored recommendations, and advisory services - all included in your SAP cloud subscription. Get started today: 

  • One-time sign up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, using a valid S-User ID (please allow up to 2 hours for processing) 
  • Access SAP Enterprise Support value maps for personalized guidance 
  • Request a call with our solution area experts for tailored advice 

Value Maps are part of SAP Enterprise Support commitment to your success. Visit the SAP Enterprise Support value maps landing page to explore our range of topics and start engaging with us today. 

 

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

About the author: 

  • Mabelle Roque Miguel is a Focus Advisor for SAP Enterprise Support value maps for SAP Supply Chain Management solutions. Based in Singapore, Mabelle has been with SAP since 2007, taking on diverse roles that have largely centered around customer engagement and success. Now, her focus is on guiding businesses through their supply chain journey, helping them accelerate the adoption of SAP supply chain management solutions with value map content and SAP Enterprise Support offerings.

Have questions, comments or feedback? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @Mabelle.

entry.title: Unlock the Future of Customer Support at SAP’s Customer Support Summit – Register Now!
entry.content:
Join us on Thursday, March 27, 2025, at SAP’s Newtown Square office for an exclusive Customer Support Summit—an event designed to inspire, inform, and connect SAP customers and partners with the latest innovations in customer support and cloud lifecycle management.

Why Attend?

Customer support is evolving rapidly, and SAP is leading the way with cutting-edge AI-powered solutions, enhanced self-service tools, and strategic support frameworks. At this summit, you'll gain insights into how SAP’s latest advancements can help you optimize operations, enhance system performance, and drive business success.

What’s in Store for You?

  • Special Guests: Geoff Scott, CEO and Chief Community Champion at ASUG and Dave Lehanski, Executive Vice President, Business Development & Innovation at NHL.

  • Keynote by Mohammed Ajouz – Get inspired by SAP’s Senior Vice President, Global Head of Technical Support, as he shares the vision for SAP Customer Support & Cloud Lifecycle Management.

  • Thought-Provoking Panel Discussion – Hear from SAP leaders, customers, and partners on the future of customer support, AI-driven automation, and the next wave of agentic support solutions.

  • Tech Talks & Demo Pods – Experience hands-on demonstrations of SAP’s self-service tools, AI-powered support solutions, and SAP Enterprise Support offerings.

  • Networking Reception – Connect with fellow professionals, industry experts, and SAP executives in an engaging and interactive setting.

Who Should Attend?

This free-of-charge event is tailored for SAP customers and partners who want to stay ahead in the ever-evolving support landscape. Whether you’re exploring RISE with SAP, GROW with SAP, or looking for ways to optimize your support strategy, this summit offers invaluable insights and direct access to SAP experts.

Don’t miss this opportunity to engage, learn, and connect with the best in the industry.

Secure your spot today – Register now!

entry.title: How Data driven Change Management with Deep Transformation Insights ensures successful SAP Business
entry.content:
This blog highlights the critical role of Organizational Change Management (OCM) in the successful implementation of SAP S/4HANA. It delves into how SAP's Deep Transformation Insights (DTI) service leverages data-driven insights to help organizations manage change effectively, ensuring employee acceptance and engagement throughout the transformation process.

The Challenge of SAP S/4HANA Implementations: A Change Management Perspective

Implementing SAP S/4HANA is a transformative process that goes beyond a mere technological upgrade. It reshapes business operations, IT systems, and employee behavior while embracing a Fit-to-Standard mindset This increased focus on standards helps to reduce the time and costs associated with implementation and support, and also drives business efficiencies as well as scalability. It is SAP’s recommended approach to enable faster adoption of SAP S/4HANA.

Despite the recognized importance of Organizational Change Management (OCM) in ensuring successful business transformation, many organizations struggle to integrate it effectively into their strategy. We recently did a Change Assessment with more than 800 respondents on the perception of different important OCM related dimensions. See here some highlights:

Key Challenges in OCM Implementations

Strategic Alignment and Ownership

  • Confusion about who should take ownership of OCM within the organization
  • Lack of clarity in defining desired business outcomes
  • Misconceptions about OCM's scope and responsibilities

Leadership Involvement

  • 64% of companies acknowledge senior leaders' crucial role in driving change
  • Only 56% report active engagement of leaders with their teams
  • Lack of visible support undermines morale and alignment

Communication and Change Story

  • Only 48% of organizations actively communicate SAP program updates
  • 54% have a clearly defined Change Story

Resistance to change evolves when discussions focus around Fit-Gap keeping the current business processes in the future system and NOT focusing on fit-to-standard.

Past Experiences and Skepticism

  • Poor outcomes in previous OCM efforts lead to skepticism
  • Some organizations exclude OCM from new projects due to past experiences

Resource Constraints and ROI Measurement

  • Lack of internal structures and expertise for effective OCM implementation
  • 71% believe their Change Management Strategy explains the SAP program's importance
  • Only 47% feel end users are equipped to apply SAP solutions in daily work
  • OCM is often viewed as an optional expense rather than a critical success factor
  • Difficulty in justifying additional spending on OCM
  • Only 44% of organizations actively assess the effectiveness of their change initiatives
  • Lack of clear metrics to demonstrate ROI

Overcoming these challenges requires a holistic approach to OCM, emphasizing clear ownership, leadership involvement, effective communication, and measurable outcomes. By addressing these key areas, organizations can maximize the benefits of their SAP S/4HANA implementation and ensure a smoother transition for all stakeholders.

To address such challenges the Deep Transformation Insights (DTI) service from SAP comes into play. With this data driven approach, organizations can safeguard the success of their SAP S/4HANA implementations. It's not just about deploying new technology; it's about preparing the people who will use it, ensuring sustainable adoption, and unlocking the full potential of the transformation.

DTI is built around five key dimensions or sentiments—Awareness, Acceptance, Enablement, Empowerment, and Commitment—that provide a structured path for guiding individuals through change, fostering both readiness and long-term engagement. Let’s look at these briefly.

Research-based Change Sentiments to Accelerate Change and Adoption in SAP Implementations

Based on research conducted by organizational scientists like A. Armenakis, this five important factors have been identified as they determine the degree of employee buy-in by organizational change recipients.

Awareness

  • Foundation of successful transformation
  • Ensures recognition of the need for change
  • Focuses on communicating the "why" behind the change
  • Aligns change initiative with broader business goals

Acceptance

  • Builds on awareness
  • Addresses emotional and intellectual readiness
  • Focuses on trust-building and reducing resistance
  • Creates psychological safety for employees

Enablement

  • Bridge between readiness and action
  • Equips individuals with necessary tools, training, and resources
  • Creates a supportive environment for adapting to new roles
  • Minimizes disruption and maximizes productivity during transformation

Empowerment

  • Fosters ownership and confidence among employees
  • Grants authority for decision-making within respective roles
  • Cultivates a culture of distributed decision-making
  • Inspires innovation and accountability at all levels

Adoption and Commitment

  • Emphasizes alignment between personal and organizational goals
  • Prioritizes intrinsic and extrinsic motivation
  • Focuses on recognition and reinforcement
  • Ensures continued engagement, real productive usage of the new processes or tools

 

From an SAP perspective, we assembled these factors and change sentiments into a framework aligned with the SAP Activate methodology and focused on SAP systems implementations.

We call this framework Deep Transformation Insights (DTI), utilizing change sentiment questionnaires throughout the implementation cycle for customers. DTI contains assessment tools that can be administered at any stage of the implementation process.

The insights obtained can serve as:

(a) An indicator of the degree of adoption/acceptance among users

(b) An assessment of issues in project enablement that can adversely impact the success of the implementation

(c) A basis for planning and executing learning to enhance user adoption

(d) A regular data driven pulse-check to monitor success of actions to improve the five dimensions.

Customer Case highlighting Activities from Data-Driven Insights to Accelerate User Adoption in SAP S/4HANA Projects

So how could this look like in SAP projects? To make it tangible we would like to share a real world customer case.   

The customer prepared for a planned implementation of SAP S/4HANA supporting an enterprise-wide digital transformation strategy to enhance scalability and efficiency. Further business alignment goals included greater simplicity, efficiency, insight, collaboration, transparency, and compliance, among others.

The company achieved success by prioritizing employee buy-in and minimizing disruption. Using SAP's Deep Transformation Insights service, they enhanced their change management with digital tools and data-driven insights. This people-centric approach effectively managed the transformation's complexity, leading to a successful implementation After implementing the DTI service, the company established and orchestrated an employee engagement feedback architecture using a structured change-sentiment framework across project and organizational units.
Additionally, they gained better insights into stakeholder perceptions and experiences throughout the project lifecycle. They now use these insights as the foundation to accelerate and support solution adoption.

"Utilizing DTI was seamless, and the insights we gained from the team were very thorough, thoughtful, and actionable. We were able to easily see that the work we were doing was having the intended effect because of the results from DTI"
Director Organizational Change Management, Kyndryl

Summary

Organizational Change Management (OCM) is crucial for ensuring successful adoption and realizing the full potential of any digital transformation. However, many organizations face challenges in integrating OCM into their implementation strategy.

Deep Transformation Insights (DTI) offers a comprehensive solution to these challenges, providing a data-driven approach to change management that aligns with the SAP Activate methodology. By focusing on five proven key sentiments—Awareness, Acceptance, Enablement, Empowerment, and Commitment—DTI helps organizations guide their employees through the change process, fostering engagement and long-term success. Find out more about SAP Organizational Change Management on sap.com, or contact us per e-mail.

We look forward to discuss your questions in the comments.

entry.title: How to activate your S-User ID
entry.content:
Log in to SAP for Me and start exploring everything the platform has to offer today! 

                                     

A company that has a support contract with SAP and a Super Administrator set up within the company can create your S-User ID. You’ll receive an automatically generated welcome e-mail. This email will allow you to activate your S-User which can then be linked to your Universal ID.   

For whatever reason your S-User activation may have expired or perhaps you accidentally deleted the activation e-mail.  

Here are the steps to follow for an alternative activation method:

  1. Go to Forgot My Password.  

  1. Enter your S-user ID and click continue.   
  2. Check your inbox for an e-mail sent from SAP ID Service (ias@notifications.sap.com) to your S-user e-mail address.  
  3. Open the e-mail and click on the password reset link.  

 To note:   

  • The password reset link is valid for 2 hours from the time that it was generated from the system and can only be used once. If the password link expires, repeat the steps 1–4.   
  • You can only change your password once in a 24-hour period. 

  Now your S-User has been activated it will be available to add to your Universal ID! 

 Check out this blog to add your newly activated to your Universal ID: SAP Universal ID: Tips and Tricks - SAP Community    

For more information on S-User ID’s take a look at this blog: Learn All About S-User IDs! - SAP Community  

 Troubleshooting Tips:   

If you haven’t received the password reset e-mail after a few minutes:  

  • Check your SPAM/junk folder.  
  • Clear your browsing history, cookies, and cache and repeat the steps. 
  • Check that your company email settings are not blocking emails from SAP.  

 If you still haven't received the e-mail: Contact the SAP ID Service Team (SSO Team) at the following email address: sso@sap.com. You'll see more details on the SSO Team here: When and How to contact the SAP ID Service Team

                                                                                                                                                       

Thanks for reading!   

We hope you found this useful. Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have. 

If you have any questions, please ask a questions in the Q & A using All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community

Read more ##CustomerInteractionCenter blog-posts here!

entry.title: Did you know: SAP Support Log Assistant 2.0 Self Service Portal? & Trace errors for your IPS logs
entry.content:
SAP Support Log Assistant 2.0 is a powerful tool designed to streamline the analysis of support-related files such as logs, traces, and configuration files. This tool leverages the expertise of SAP Product Support to provide automated solutions and recommendations for known issues.

Try it now - Support log assistant standalone, self-service tool

SAP Video - Support Log Assistant - Self Service Tool Demo

SAP Knowledge Base Article: 2838708

How It Helps: The Support Log Assistant 2.0 helps users by:

  1. Automated Analysis: It scans text-based files for known issues and provides recommendations based on SAP's extensive knowledge base
  2. Efficient Troubleshooting: By identifying and highlighting important details in the files, it accelerates issue resolution without the need to open a support case
  3. Time-Saving: Reduces the manual effort required for log analysis and solution searches, allowing users to focus on other critical tasks

Benefits and Advantages:

  1. 24/7 Availability: The tool is available round-the-clock, providing expert-level log file analysis at any time
  2. Accelerated Resolution: Speeds up the process of resolving issues by automatically identifying problems and suggesting solutions
  3. PDF Reports: Generates detailed PDF reports of log analysis findings and recommendations, which can be shared with peers to extend best practices
  4. Standalone Access: Users can access the tool as a standalone application via the SAP for Me customer portal
  5. Enhanced Collaboration: Facilitates sharing of analysis results with peers, promoting collaborative problem-solving

Use Cases:

  1. Enterprise Data Management: Analyzing log files related to data management systems to identify and resolve issues quickly
  2. SAP Identity Provisioning Logs : Analyzing provisioning job files downloaded to identify and resolve issues quickly with Source & Target Systems defined or Transformation logic issues
  3. HANA Platform: Troubleshooting HANA database-related problems by scanning configuration and trace files
  4. Business Intelligence: Ensuring the health of BI systems by automatically detecting and addressing log file errors
  5. Big Data: Managing and resolving issues in big data environments through automated log analysis
  6. NW JAVA and NW Core: Supporting NetWeaver Java and Core systems by providing solutions to log file issues.

FAQs for Support Log Assistant

SAP Identity Provisioning Service - Provisioning Logs -  Download Logs to analyze

After uploading the log files, Scanning will start and provide you the initial analysis with list of Alerts which is causing the issue of failed and also provides SAP Note KBA details as well

Summary will provide the graphic representation to Download Analysis Report 

Conclusion: 

The SAP Support Log Assistant is an invaluable tool for customers, Partners & SAP users, offering automated, expert-level analysis of support-related files. Its ability to accelerate issue resolution, save time, and facilitate collaboration makes it a must-have for maintaining a healthy SAP environment.

entry.title: Exposing a RESTful Parameterized Service Using OO ABAP and SICF, Tested with Postman
entry.content:
In this blog, we will walk through the steps to expose a REST-based parameterized service in ABAP using SICF and a custom handler class. This will help in handling HTTP requests and returning appropriate responses.

Steps to Create the REST Service

Define the Service in SICF

  1. Navigate to Transaction SICF.

Execute (F8) → Navigate to default_host → sap → bc.

Right-click on bc → Click on New Sub-Element.In the popup window, click OK.Enter the service name and click OK.

 

In the Service Data tab, provide the necessary details.

 

Navigate to the Logon Data tab and configure authentication settings.

 

Navigate to the Handler List tab → Enter the service handler class name: ZCL_EXP_PARM_SRV.

 

Click Save. 

You may see a message indicating that the handler class does not exist yet. This will be created in the next step.

 

Create the Handler Class

Navigate to Transaction SE24.
Create a class named ZCL_EXP_PARM_SRV.

 

Navigate to the Interfaces tab → Enter the interface name: IF_HTTP_EXTENSION.

 

Navigate to the Attributes tab → Add the attribute _HTTP_SERVER.

Navigate to the Methods tab → Locate the method IF_HTTP_EXTENSION~HANDLE_REQUEST.

Double-click on the method name, then save and activate the class.

Activate the Service in SICF

 

Implement the Method IF_HTTP_EXTENSION~HANDLE_REQUEST

To process incoming HTTP requests, implement the method IF_HTTP_EXTENSION~HANDLE_REQUEST in your handler class. This method will:

  • Read the incoming payload.

  • Process parameters.

  • Return the appropriate response.

 

 

  METHOD if_http_extension~handle_request.
    " Define error structure
    TYPES : BEGIN OF ty_error,
              type    TYPE char1,
              message TYPE char50,
            END OF ty_error.

    " Define variables
    DATA : respons_data_ TYPE string,                     " Response data in JSON format
           _t_error      TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_error, " Error table
           o_response_   TYPE REF TO if_http_response,    " HTTP Response object
           _o_exception  TYPE REF TO cx_root.            " Exception object

    FIELD-SYMBOLS : <fs_outtab> TYPE ANY TABLE.  " Generic table field symbol

    " Assign HTTP server instance
    _http_server  = server.

    TRY.
        " Parse the request payload
        DATA(_request_data) = server->request->get_cdata( ).
        
        " Get the HTTP method type (GET, POST, etc.)
        DATA(_method) = server->request->get_header_field( if_http_header_fields_sap=>request_method ).
        
        " Retrieve query string parameters
        DATA(_parameters) = server->request->get_header_field( if_http_header_fields_sap=>query_string ).

        " Check if parameters exist
        IF _parameters IS NOT INITIAL.
          " Extract the parameter name and value
          SPLIT _parameters AT '=' INTO DATA(_fieldname) DATA(_fieldval).

          " Fetch data from SFLIGHT table based on parameter value
          SELECT * FROM sflight INTO TABLE @DATA(_t_sflight)
            WHERE carrid EQ @_fieldval.
          
          " Assign the result to field symbol
          ASSIGN _t_sflight TO <fs_outtab>.
          
          " Handle case when no data is found
          IF sy-subrc IS NOT INITIAL.
            _t_error = VALUE #( BASE _t_error ( type = 'E' message = 'No data found' ) ).
            ASSIGN _t_error TO <fs_outtab>.
          ENDIF.
        ELSE.
          " Populate error message when parameters are missing
          _t_error = VALUE #( BASE _t_error ( type = 'E' message = 'Empty Parameters' ) ).
          ASSIGN _t_error TO <fs_outtab>.
        ENDIF.

        " Convert the output table to JSON format
        IF <fs_outtab> IS ASSIGNED.
          respons_data_ = /ui2/cl_json=>serialize(
                              data        = <fs_outtab>
                              compress    = abap_false
                              pretty_name = /ui2/cl_json=>pretty_mode-camel_case ).
          
          " Prepare HTTP response
          o_response_ = _http_server->response.
          o_response_->set_status( code = '200' reason = 'OK' ).
          o_response_->set_content_type( 'application/json' ).
          o_response_->set_cdata( respons_data_ ).
        ELSE.
          " Set response status to 500 when no data is available
          o_response_->set_status( code = '500' reason = 'Internal Server Error' ).
        ENDIF.
      
      " Handle exceptions
      CATCH cx_root INTO _o_exception.
        _http_server->response->set_status( code = 500 reason = 'Internal Server Error' ).
    ENDTRY.
  ENDMETHOD.

 

 

After implementing the logic, save and activate the method and the class.

Testing the Service

To test the service, use Postman or any other REST client.

If SFLIGHT Table is Empty

If the SFLIGHT table has no data, execute the program SAPBC_DATA_GENERATOR to generate sample data.

Instead of sending a payload, pass values through URL parameters. The request body should remain empty.

 Output

Conclusion
By following these steps, you can effectively expose a REST parameterized service in ABAP. This approach enables seamless communication using HTTP requests and responses, facilitating integration with external systems. Additionally, it enhances interoperability and ensures efficient data exchange within SAP environments.

entry.title: SAP Learning and Adoption Forum 2025 - Save the Date
entry.content:
Künstliche Intelligenz und Cloud-Technologien verändern unsere Arbeitswelt zunehmend. Doch die bloße Einführung neuer Technologien reicht nicht aus. Um das volle Potenzial von SAP-Lösungen auszuschöpfen, braucht es das Zusammenspiel von Technologie und Mensch. Wissen, Akzeptanz, Commitment, Befähigung, sprich effektives Change Management sind entscheidend. 

Das SAP Learning and Adoption Forum 2025 unter dem Motto ACTIVATE HUMAN SUPERPOWERS! widmet sich genau diesem Thema. Am 25. Juni treffen sich SAP-Experten, Kunden und Partner in Walldorf (oder online), um gemeinsam zu erkunden, wie wir Lernen und Wandel in SAP-Projekten neu denken und so die Adoption nachhaltig verbessern. Dieser Blog-Beitrag gibt einen Vorgeschmack auf das Event und zeigt, warum Ihr dabei sein solltet. 

Activate Human Superpowers: Was erwartet Euch 2025?   

Im Fokus steht dieses Jahr die Frage, wie wir unsere "menschlichen Superkräfte" aktivieren können, um die digitale Transformation erfolgreich zu gestalten.  

  • Impulse durch Lightning Talks: Erfahrt von führenden Experten wie Jochen Robes vom Weiterbildungsblog oder Timo Deiner, CTO SAP MEE, wie Lernen und Wandel strategisch verankert werden kann. 
  • Good Practices: Lernt von anderen Unternehmen in 30-minütigen Knowledge Cafes, wie sie die Adoption von SAP-Lösungen erfolgreich vorantreiben. 
  • Networking: Knüpft wertvolle Kontakte zu SAP-Experten, Kunden und Partnern.  
  • Vorabend-Event: Verpasst nicht das Vorabend-Event am 24. Juni ab 18:30 Uhr mit einem Interview der Olympiasiegerin Yemisi Ogunleye und ersten Networking-Möglichkeiten (onsite only). 

Um die Interaktion und die Co-Creation der Teilnehmenden zu fördern, bieten wir dieses Jahr erstmalig 3 co-creative, interaktive Formate an: 

  • Ein Promtathon zum „Hands-On“ Erlernen und Ausprobieren von KI Skills 
  • Ein Workshop zur Erkundung und Exploration von KI Use-Cases im Learning und Change Management 
  • Ein Workshop zu Good Practices & Lessons Learned von SAP S/4HANA Transformationen 

Zielgruppe des Forums  

Das SAP Learning and Adoption Forum richtet sich an SAP-Experten, Change Manager:Innen, Projektleiter:Innen, Personalentwickler:Innen, Weiterbildungsverantwortliche und alle, die sich für die erfolgreiche Implementierung und Nutzung von SAP-Lösungen interessieren. Egal, ob Ihr Kunde oder Partner seid – das Forum bietet wertvolle Einblicke und Kontakte. 

Rückblick auf das Forum 2024: Future Learning, Leading Change   

Das SAP Training and Adoption Forum 2024 stand unter dem Motto "Future Learning, Leading Change". In Walldorf und virtuell kamen Experten zusammen, um sich über die neuesten Trends und Best Practices auszutauschen. Der Austausch in Knowledge Cafés und Impulsvorträge bot eine Plattform für den Wissensaustausch und die Vernetzung, die von allen sehr  geschätzt wurde. Wer das Event verpasst hat oder Inhalte noch einmal Revue passieren möchte, findet eine Zusammenfassung und alle Aufzeichnungen hier. 

Anmeldung und weitere Informationen zum SAP Learning und Change Forum

Seit Ihr bereit, Eure Superkräfte zu aktivieren? Dann besucht die Event-Seite für weitere Informationen und zur Anmeldung. Die ersten Teilgebenden stehen bereits fest.   

Und wer noch Anregungen hat - meldet Euch gerne hier in den Kommentaren. Auch suchen wir noch Community MitgliederInnen die uns bei unterschiedlichen Dingen unterstützen. Sei es Organisation oder auch bei den Workshops.

entry.title: SAP Innovation Day: Beobachtungen aus der Perspektive Organisational Change Management und Training
entry.content:
Letzte Woche fand der SAP Innovation Day statt: Zum Motto Business Unleashed, Fortschritt erleben folgten eine Menge an Entscheidungsträgern und Experten der Einladung von Sven Mulder, Geschäftsführer von SAP Deutschland. 

Der Fokus der Veranstaltung lag auf der Einführung neuer Technologien, wie zum Beispiel der neuen SAP Business Suite  und wie SAP KI, Daten und Apps auf bisher nie dagewesene Weise zusammengeführt. Verdeutlicht wurde dies anhand von Praxisbeispielen von SAP Kunden, die ihre Transformationsprojekte vorstellten.Dabei wurden in fast allen Vorträgen und Sessions die Themen Change Management, Lernen und Training als unerlässliche Faktoren für nachhaltigen Erfolg in der Transformation genannt.

Schon in der Keynote von Wirtschaftsweise Monika Schnitzer wurde betont, wie wichtig Veränderungsbereitschaft, Reskilling und lebenslanges Lernen in Zeiten der digitalen Transformation sind. Diese Themen wurden in den folgenden Sessions immer wieder aufgegriffen und durch konkrete Beispiele untermauert.

Die dmTECH GmbH setzt Hackathons und Reverse Mentoring ein, um den Wissensaustausch und die Innovationskraft im Unternehmen zu fördern.

Bei MEDICE Arzneimittel wurde deutlich, wie wichtig ein inspirierender Projekt-Kickoff ist, um den richtigen "Spirit" ins Team zu bringen und Change Management von Anfang an zu verankern.

Acceleron demonstrierte mit ihrem "IT Change Accelerator", wie sie 23 SAP-Systeme auf 3 reduzierten - ein Mammutprojekt, das nur durch intensives Organisational Change Management nachhaltig gelingen konnte. Auch Bayer betonte, wie wichtig es ist, die Mitarbeitenden mitzunehmen und die Gründe für Veränderungen transparent zu kommunizieren.

Hymer unterstrich die Bedeutung von Risiko- und Stakeholder-Analysen für erfolgreiche Transformationsprojekte, während Mahle durch OCM Maßnahmen und Enablement in ihre Mitarbeitenden investiert, um sie für die Reise der Transformation zu befähigen. Daimler Truck setzte 86 Trainingseinheiten für 7‘000 Nutzer:innen auf- ein klares Bekenntnis zur Wichtigkeit von Weiterbildung im Transformationsprozess.

Schlussgedanke
Der SAP Innovation Day verdeutlichte die Dringlichkeit, Transformation nicht nur als technischen Fortschritt, sondern als ganzheitlichen organisatorischen Wandel zu sehen, der starkes Change Management und kontinuierliches Lernen erfordert. Die klare Botschaft des SAP Innovation Day: Investitionen in Change Management und Weiterbildung sind keine nice-to have Option, sondern eine Notwendigkeit für erfolgreiche digitale Transformationsprojekte.

Indem Unternehmen in diese Bereiche investieren, können sie ihre Mitarbeitenden für die Herausforderungen der Zukunft rüsten und sicherstellen, dass technologische Innovationen effektiv und nachhaltig implementiert werden. Wir freuen uns sehr, dass diese Themen die wir seit Jahren vertreten, nun immer wichtiger werden.,

Für alle, die sich weiter mit dem Thema beschäftigen möchten, bieten wir verschiedene Ressourcen:

  1. Unser jähreliches Treffen – das SAP Learning and Adoption Forum in Walldorf und online am 25.6. https://go4.events.sap.com/eur-learning-adoption-forum/de_de/home.html
  2. Monatlicher Austausch in der SAP Change und Training Kaffee Ecke https://go4.events.sap.com/eur-change-training-kaffee-ecke/de_de/home.html
  3. Infos auf sap.com: SAP Organizational Change Management (OCM): https://www.sap.com/products/technology-platform/cloud-platform/organizational-change-management.html
  4. SAP Community Gruppe zu Training und Change Management: https://community.sap.com/t5/sap-training-and-change-management/gh-p/training-change
    LinkedIn Gruppe zu SAP Training und Change: https://www.linkedin.com/groups/8682361/
  5. SAP Learning Hub: https://www.sap.com/products/learning-hub.html
  6. Newsletter für SAP Schulungen: https://www.sap.com/germany/cmp/nl/sap-education-newsletter/typ.html
  7.  SAP Lernangebot: https://www.learning.sap.com
  8. SAP Kurskatalog: https://training.sap.com/training-course-schedule
  9. SAP Change Management Basecamp – der Workshop zu Change Management:: https://training.sap.com/course/s4cm01-vernderungsmanagement-erlernen-und-anwenden-am-praxisnahen-beispiel-classroom-001-de-en/?

entry.title: O Captain! My Captain! Agentic AI Leads the Way in the Digital Age
entry.content:
One of the most enriching human experiences takes place in an educational facility. There, students learn soft skills and theoretical background from their teachers, which are the first steps toward becoming a trustworthy professional. Just as students learn and apply knowledge to real-world scenarios, agentic AI systems learn from data inputs to perform tasks autonomously, embodying a modern interpretation of this learning-teaching dynamic. As these systems are designed to achieve complex areas with limited commands, there is a growing demand for tools that learn from input and provide results with thorough reasoning.

Agentic AI Capabilities

Agentic AI combines large language models, machine learning, and natural language processing to carry out autonomous activities on behalf of the user. It is embedded with self-organizing capabilities and self-learning features, which include:

AI-driven support across all case handling stages – It’s intended to be implemented at multiple stages of the case handling process, from information and log collection to triage, troubleshooting, analysis, and action recommendation.

Enhanced efficiency and experience - The goal is to considerably reduce customer effort and engineer workload while improving case resolution times and overall satisfaction.

Automation of repetitive tasks - Repetitive tasks such as writing emails, summarizing cases, and searching for past solutions are automated with AI, allowing agents to focus on problem-solving. Within SAP systems, it can analyze situations, formulate strategies, and execute actions autonomously using SAP Build Process Automation. Just as budding professionals apply tools and methods for learning, agentic AI accelerates task proficiency by providing real-time, context-aware answers to new support engineers. This capability narrows the distance between knowledge acquisition and effective application.

AI-assisted communication - AI-assisted responses help agents craft clearer, more customer-friendly messages, leading to stronger customer satisfaction. AI-generated responses also allow agents to maintain the right tone and empathy in emotionally charged cases and helps non-native speakers communicate more clearly.

Proactive and preventative support - AI is used to detect and resolve system issues proactively. Preventative Customer Care is expanded across all products and integrated with PARIM to bring a true end-to-end Preventative Risk Identification, Risk Mitigation, and Risk Tracking solution.

Flexible architecture - A flexible architecture is in place for consumption of the upcoming SAP Agentic Framework. Based on perception, decision-making, learning, and action components, it enables adaptation over time through implementation of safety measures through monitored performance.

Continuous improvement - There is an evaluation of current existing data, data pipelines, and AI Services to identify areas of growth and continuous improvement.

Best Next Action recommendation - Agentic support offers engineers the ability to get Best Next Action recommendations from a chain of agents and trigger agents at will using prompts to solve cases. The overarching aim is to introduce AI and automation to offer intelligent insights and reduce engineer effort on case resolution. It doesn’t replace human support but rather augments their capabilities, making the learning-teaching dynamic more effective and efficient in the digital age.

Smart response capability - The implementation of smart response capability offers AI-generated responses to selective customer cases, to reduce the volume of cases processed by engineers and enable deflection of cases through Smart Respond. The aim is to auto-respond to selective cases, and constantly increase the proportion of cases that we can Smart Respond to by deriving the confidence from responses offered by engineers to trivial cases, where they are using the Best Next Action recommendations, as-is.

While generative AI primarily creates content, agentic AI focuses on decision-making and actions, making it action-oriented. Furthermore, this customer-centric approach provides nuanced, conversational support beyond traditional chatbots, and offers personalized recommendations and experiences.

The Path Ahead for Agentic AI Integration

The foresight looks promising, with developments for agentic AI in SAP products that encompass:

  1. Connection of support agents with self-healing and reasoning agents to fix issues automatically.
  2. Improvement of natural language interfaces with SAP products, allowing real-time insights about business processes.
  3. Integration with emerging technologies like Internet of Things and blockchain.

As in an educational system, agentic AI is set to learn from data to make informed decisions. At SAP Sapphire 2025, to take place in Orlando and Madrid, we’ll showcase how our agents identify and mitigate risks to prevent business impact to customers, as we point towards a future of seamless integration between learning and problem-solving skills.

A special thanks to Bharath Ramamurthy, Vice President of Strategic Initiatives, Customer Support, and Tarun Luthra, Global Head of Technical Support, Industries & Customer Experience, SAP experts who have contributed to this article.

entry.title: Treffen Sie die Experten des SAP Kundensupports auf den DSAG-Technologietagen 2025 in Wiesbaden
entry.content:
Treffen Sie die Experten des SAP Kundensupports auf den DSAG-Technologietagen 2025 in Wiesbaden

Vom 2. bis 3. April 2025 ist es wieder soweit – die DSAG-Technologietage öffnen ihre Türen in Wiesbaden, und der SAP-Kundensupport ist vor Ort mit spannenden Inhalten und innovativen Ansätzen vertreten! Besuchen Sie den SAP-Stand und lernen Sie die Experten des SAP Kundensupports persönlich kennen.

Was erwartet Sie am SAP-Stand?

Unsere Experten decken eine Vielzahl an Themen ab, die für die Transformation und den Betrieb Ihrer SAP-Lösungen relevant sind:

  • Integrated Toolchain – Umstellung auf die Cloud oder auf eine Clean Core Strategie mit Hilfe von SAP Cloud ALM, SAP Signavio, LeanIX
  • SAP Cloud ALM – Optimieren Sie Ihre Cloud-Reise mit proaktiver Überwachung und Prozessunterstützung
  • SAP Business Transformation Center & Transformation Tools – Tools zur nahtlosen Datenmigration und Systemtransformation
  • AI & Agentic AI im Kundensupport – KI-getriebene Innovationen, die Support-Prozesse revolutionieren
  • SAP for Me & Early Watch Services – Optimieren Sie Ihren Systembetrieb und bleiben Sie proaktiv informiert
  • Black Friday Cyber Monday / Holiday Season Readiness Program – Innovative Tools wie Smart Resolve und Cure 360 zur Vorbereitung auf die umsatzstärksten Verkaufsevents des Jahres

Nutzen Sie die Gelegenheit, um mit den SAP-Experten ins Gespräch zu kommen, und erfahren Sie, wie der SAP-Kundensupport als Frontrunner im Einsatz von Künstlicher Intelligenz (KI), Agentic AI und LLMs (Large Language Models) vorangeht. Erhalten Sie Einblicke in den praktischen Einsatz dieser Technologien und nehmen Sie wertvolle Ansätze für Ihr Unternehmen mit.

Verpassen Sie nicht diese spannenden Sessions!

In Ihrem Messefahrplan sollten diese beiden Sessions auf keinen Fall fehlen:

Session 1: SAP TED Talk
🗓2. April, 17:15–18:00 Uhr Sichern Sie Ihre Reise in die Cloud mit SAP Cloud ALM
Planen Sie den Umstieg auf die Cloud? SAP Cloud ALM bietet Ihnen eine klare Roadmap und umfassende Unterstützung für Ihre Cloud-Reise. Diese Lösung minimiert Risiken und stellt einen nahtlosen Betriebsablauf sicher. Entdecken Sie durch praxisnahe Demonstrationen die neuesten Funktionen und erhalten Sie entscheidende Einblicke, um Ihre Cloud-Strategie effektiv zu optimieren. Mit SAP Cloud ALM erhalten Sie nicht nur technische Unterstützung, sondern auch klare Anleitungen für das Projektmanagement, um Ihren Unternehmenserfolg in der digitalen Transformation zu sichern.

Sprecher: Sören Ruder, SVP, Chief Product Officer (CPO) and Head of Customer Experience & Solutions (CXS)

Session 2: SAP Expert Session (freie Q&A)
🗓3. April, 10:55–11:15 Uhr Autonomes Application Lifecycle Management
Die Rolle der Künstlichen Intelligenz (KI), insbesondere der Agentischen KI, beim Transformieren des Application Lifecycle Managements (ALM) steht im Zentrum dieser Präsentation. Erfahren Sie, wie KI-Technologien nicht nur Aufgaben automatisieren, sondern auch proaktiv Entscheidungen treffen, um die Effizienz und Leistungsfähigkeit Ihrer IT-Systeme zu steigern. Erleben Sie, wie autonomes ALM herkömmliche Verwaltungsansätze revolutioniert und den Weg für innovative, zukunftsweisende Prozessgestaltung ebnet.

Sprecher: Sören Ruder, SVP, Chief Product Officer (CPO) and Head of Customer Experience & Solutions (CXS)

Beachten Sie bitte auch folgende Sessions:

2. April 2025

  • 13:00 Uhr - V005: SAP ALM Überblick und Ausblick
  • 13:00 Uhr - V021: SAP EarlyWatch Alert: kritische Alerts Ihrer hybriden SAP-Landschaft zentral zusammengefasst - auch nach dem Wartungsende des SAP Solution Managers
  • 14:15 Uhr - V006: SAP Cloud ALM im Betrieb
  • 14:15 Uhr - V022: KI Szenarien im SAP Services & Support / Mit der AI Innovation Plattform den Business Value von SAP Services & Support steigern
  • 16:00 - V007: SAP Cloud ALM für Implementierung
  • 17:15 - V008: SAP Maintenance Strategy - End of Mainstream Maintenance SAP Solution Manager 7.2. - what you need to know now

3. April 2025

  • 10:30 Uhr - V057: Transition nach SAP Cloud ALM
  • 14:00 Uhr - V059: SAP Cloud ALM - Change & Deployment Management
  • 15:15 Uhr - V060: SAP Cloud ALM im Kontext Rise und AI 

Treffen Sie uns auch in den DSAG-Arbeitskreisen

Unsere Experten sind darüber hinaus in den DSAG-Arbeitskreisen aktiv. Ein Blick in die Agenda lohnt sich – vielleicht sehen wir uns dort!

Wir freuen uns auf den persönlichen Austausch mit Ihnen in Wiesbaden. Lassen Sie uns gemeinsam über Ihre individuellen Herausforderungen sprechen und wie wir Sie mit modernen Tools, KI-Innovationen und bewährten Services auf Ihrer Reise zur Cloud und darüber hinaus begleiten können.

Mehr Informationen und die vollständige Agenda finden Sie auf der offiziellen Website der DSAG-Technologietage.

entry.title: Unlock the Power of SAP Universal ID: Simplify Your Digital Identity
entry.content:
First let’s understand what SAP Universal ID is and what are the benefits of having one. 



Are you finding it challenging to manage numerous SAP accounts and passwords? Imagine having a single, unified account that links all your existing company associations, allowing you to switch between them effortlessly. That's where SAP Universal ID comes in! 

 

SAP Universal ID will be your personal SAP identity, the S-user ID is a Business SAP identity given by company administrators and can be utilized as an entitlement under the SAP Universal ID.


With SAP Universal ID, you can: 
 

  • Take control of your experience: Own and manage your personal SAP identity. 

 

  • Keep your account for life: Use it wherever you go, regardless of where you work. 

 

  • Use one login for all your accounts: Manage one account and password by linking all your existing company accounts (S-user IDs) in one universal account.  


Once your account is set up, you can access the SAP Universal ID Account Manager, which is your single-entry point to view, update, and manage your personal information, account security settings, and associations to companies. 

 
Here is a link to the video https://www.sap.com/assetdetail/2023/03/d0944279-6a7e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html and see how simple it is to create SAP Universal ID. 

 

SAP Universal ID will also preserve users' community reputations and learning certificates. This ensures the continuity of personal achievements across SAP platforms and services, reinforcing its pivotal role in user identity management within the SAP ecosystem. 

Benefits of having SAP Universal ID: 

  • SAP Universal ID allows you to combine all your existing and future S/P-user accounts under a single password, eliminating the need to keep track of all your S/P-user IDs. 
  • SAP Universal ID includes all your existing and future S/P-user identities. In the SAP Universal ID Account Manager, you can manage all your user identities, personal data, security, and privacy settings 
  • Your SAP Universal ID is personal and belongs to you throughout your working life. You can use it to preserve your learning certificates and community reputation even when you change job. 
  • In general, it is not mandatory to create SAP Universal ID. However, if you want to preserve your community reputations and learning certificates, you will need to have an SAP Universal ID. 

Now it has been made mandate to add a secondary email ID under SAP Universal ID to ensure that your SAP Universal ID is compliant. 

 
Here is the link to add recovery email to your SAP Universal ID - https://learninghub.sap.com/help-center/faq/uidlink and https://learning.sap.com/how-to-assign-a-second-email-account 

 

If you are ever in doubt, please contact the Customer Interaction Center team CIC 

If you wish to unlink a secondary email ID from your primary SAP Universal ID or delete your SAP Universal ID entirely, you can contact CIC via email with the necessary details. A confirmation email, including all your linked email IDs, is required to proceed. This ensures a written record of your request and helps prevent any discrepancies. 
 

Steps to Unlink a Secondary Email ID: 

  • UID (email ID created via account.sap.com) : 
  • E-mail, or S/P IDs to be unlinked: 
  • Reason: 
  • Proof of ownership (The request must be sent from the UID email address, or alternatively from an email account linked to it): 
  • Please confirm if you have any certifications linked to your SAP Universal ID.

If your inquiry pertains to SAP certifications and exams, please contact our SAP Training/Certification team by following the below steps: 

 

  • You would first need to login to https://learning.sap.com
    • Scroll towards the bottom and click on Get Support under learning support. 
    • Again, scroll down towards the bottom look for Create a new case or check the status of an existing one 
    • Once done, you will be asked to enter the details and that should be sent to the training team, additionally you can check the details of yours existing training cases.

      Thanks for reading!    
         
      We would love to hear from you, let us know what you think? 

       If you have any questions, please feel free to ask us in the Q&A All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community    

       Read more #Customer Interaction Centre blogposts Here!

entry.title: SAP Signavio : How to Get Support
entry.content:
SAP Signavio Support:

If you encounter any challenges while using SAP Signavio, there are multiple support channels available to assist you. Below is a guide on how to seek assistance.

  1. For product related questions open a ticket in SAP for Me.

The SAP for Me portal provides access to task-driven support resources and applications. Within the Services and Support section, you will find multiple options to connect with SAP Signavio service experts. You can explore the knowledge base for existing solutions or use the Get Support tile to create a case for further assistance.

 

Important Note:

To access support, you must have a valid contract with SAP active maintenance. Additionally, an S-User ID with the necessary authorizations is required to submit a support case for SAP Signavio.

For more details on obtaining an S-User ID, refer to the following resources:

  1. 3315692 - How can I create new S-User or authorizations for SAP Signavio products in SAP For Me? - SAP for Me

  2. SAP Support Portal

  3. SAP Note 1271482

    For comprehensive guidance, please refer to the official help documentation:
    👉SAP Signavio Support | SAP Help Portal

2. For personalized guidance or Assistance for SAP Enterprise Support Academy and value maps

Once you've signed up you can directly access our learning platform via:

SAP Enterprise Support Academy     
SAP Signavio Value Map

If you require assistance with on how to utilize SAP Signavio onboarding and adoption offerings, use the form provided to raise a call back request or you can also write to  contact us via email.

3. Academic support for SAP Signavio


For assistance, reach out to the SAP Signavio support team through the Contact Information page.

 

4. For questions on SAP for Me navigation, S-user ID and Universal ID contact the Customer Interaction Center. 


5. For non-technical questions contact your Account Executive or Customer Success Partner. 

Important Notes-

SAP Signavio Support | SAP Help Portal

SAP Signavio Onboarding Resource Center


General SAP notes：

SAP Signavio Support | SAP Help Portal

Contact Information | Signavio


We hope you found this useful. Please note that the Customer Interaction Center is always happy to guide you on any of the mentioned processes and assist you with any queries you may have. 

If you have any questions, please post in the Q&A (Question & Answer) section here: All Questions in Customer Experience | SAP Community and All Questions in Support Services | SAP Community 

Read more #Customer Interaction Centre blogposts Here!

entry.title: SAP Universal ID: 邮箱已绑定情况下需解绑
entry.content:
本博客由我的同事 Vivian Cheng 翻译

遇到无法绑定 SAP Universal ID 情况，并出现以下报错

根据知识库文章 2914815 - 如何从 SAP 通用 ID 中解除用户的绑定或删除 UID - SAP for Me，

客户有两个可选择的操作步骤：

如果您有 S-User，您可以在 SAP For Me 创建案例

  1. 创建案例：

选择组件 XX-SER-UID 创建案例，并提供以下信息：

  • SAP Universal ID 主邮箱：
  • 删除SAP Universal ID：是/否
  • 解绑 (S-user ID, P-user ID)：
  • 移除邮箱：
  • 请求理由：

案例必须由绑定到 SAP Universal ID的 S-user 或SAP Universal ID主邮箱所属的客户编号下的超级，云或用户管理员创建。

如果您没有S-User 或无法在 SAP For Me 上创建案例，可以联系我们的客户互动中心

 

  1. 联系客户互动中心（CIC）：

通过电子邮件将您的请求发送至客户互动中心，并提供以下信息：

  • SAP Universal ID 主邮箱：
  • 删除SAP Universal ID：是/否
  • 解绑 (S-user ID, P-user ID)：
  • 移除邮箱：
  • 请求理由：

 

电子邮件必须从与SAP Universal ID绑定的电子邮箱发送和/或您请求解绑的账户 (S-user ID, P-user ID) 对应的注册邮箱发送。

 

请查看您所在地的客户互动中心电子邮箱：

  • 中国，香港，印度尼西亚，马来西亚，巴基斯坦，菲律宾，台湾，泰国，韩国，新加坡和越南：support.asia@sap.com
  • 澳大利亚和新西兰：support.australia@sap.com
  • 奥地利，比利时，捷克共和国，丹麦，芬兰，法国，德国，希腊，匈牙利，爱尔兰，以色列，意大利，卢森堡，荷兰，挪威，波兰，葡萄牙，巴基斯坦，斯洛伐克，南非，西班牙，瑞典，瑞士和英国：support.emea@sap.com
  • 印度：support.india@sap.com
  • 日本：support.japan@sap.com
  • 安第斯，墨西哥，巴西和南部地区：support.latinamerica@sap.com
  • 卡塔尔，科威特，阿联酋，阿曼，约旦，巴林，埃及，沙特阿拉伯，也门，叙利亚，利比亚，黎巴嫩，巴勒斯坦，伊拉克和苏丹：support.mena@sap.com
  • 美国和加拿大：support.usca@sap.com / support.emea@sap.com

 

学习和认证

可通过创建案例联系Learning team，具体操作步骤请参阅如何联系 SAP 教育 - 学习中心团队以获取支持

 

如果您已在 SAP 学习中心完成培训，您可能已收到带有学习证书的徽章。

 

支持团队会检查 SAP Universal ID，确认学习认证是否已分配给您的 SAP Universal ID 账户。

 

如果学习认证已分配：在学习认证成功转移到其他账户前不支持删除SAP Universal ID（参阅 KBA 2547250 和 2764910）

 

如果学习认证未分配：所有信息都核对无误的情况下将删除SAP Universal ID

 

无论学习认证是否已分配，仅解绑 S或 P用户不会影响分配给特定用户或SAP Universal ID的学习认证。

 

在请求删除SAP Universal ID前，请确保查看 KBA 3383999 了解合规的删除理由。

SAP Universal ID一旦由相关团队删除，将是永久性的，不可逆操作。请注意，删除 SAP Universal ID也会影响您的 SAP Community账号。删除SAP Universal ID将同时删除您的 SAP Community账号。

 

您也可以通过 SAP Universal ID Account Manager请求删除SAP Universal ID。需点击屏幕左侧的 “隐私”，然后点击“请求账户删除”（参阅 https://account.sap.com/manage/privacy）

 

谢谢参阅！

我们希望这些信息对您有帮助。请注意，客户互动中心随时乐意为您提供上述流程的指导。

我们期待听到您的反馈意见，让我们了解您的想法。

 

相关文章

29https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs-about-sap-websites/sap-uids-zusammenf%C3%BChren/ba-p/1395979048...- 常见问题解答 - SAP Universal User ID (SAP UID)

SAP Universal ID information 页面

相关博客德文版 - https://community.sap.com/t5/blogs-about-sap-websites/sap-universal-id-aufheben-der-verkn%C3%BCpfung...

entry.title: How to contact Fieldglass Support
entry.content:
To get support for SAP Fieldglass, access  SAP Fieldglass Help Center  here  .  

The next step is to select one of the four roles from  Worker, Supplier, Buyer, or PMO.  

From here follow the below steps  

  1. Go to Fieldglass Help Center  
  2. Choose a role that describes them 

 3. Choose a role that describes them 

 4. Locate the tab ‘Contact Us’ 

 5. Enter search terms related to their query 

 6. Check the results for recommended solutions & answers 

 7. Click the option “Something Else” if they can’t find what they are looking for 

 

 

 8. Click “Create a Case” 

 9. Fill in the case details 

 10. Click to submit the case 

Choose a support channel to submit the case. The channel can be either email or phone.  

Please be advised that phone support is available in English only. 

Following submission, case visibility and case update is available through the contact us tab.  

You will be able to see your opened cases provide an update or request a follow up by email or phone. Follow ups that you have provided through the Fieldglass Help Center would be listed under the "Follow Up" Tab. 

 

If still unsure of the process, please see https://video.sap.com/media/t/1_oi1ryvmu. This is a step by step that will assist (How-to video (40sec, no audio) . 

 S-User ID? 

SAP Fieldglass - For technical assistance 

With a valid S-user ID with the correct authorizations assigned, please create a case via the SAP for Me portal. 

If you do not have an S-user ID and you are a Worker, Supplier, Buyer, or a PMO user you can receive technical assistance via the Fieldglass Help Center as mentioned above. 

  • 1296527 - How to create a support case for SAP Support - SAP for Me 
  • 3367715 - Changes to SAP Fieldglass phone and email support channels 

Furthermore, please find below this link which you may find helpful: 

Business End User SAP Fieldglass 

Thanks for reading!

entry.title: Informationen und Updates zu SAP Preferred Success 2025
entry.content:
NEWS 2025 - Informationen und Updates zu SAP Preferred Success

"Digital Adoption" ist in aller Munde – aber was bedeutet das konkret für Unternehmen? Mein Kollege Thomas Jenewein hat in seinem aufschlussreichen Blogbeitrag die verschiedenen Aspekte und betroffenen Bereiche dieses Konzepts anschaulich dargelegt.

Ein Kernaspekt der digitalen Adoption ist die Anpassungsfähigkeit an neue technologische Möglichkeiten. Anders ausgedrückt: Es geht darum, wie Unternehmen neue digitale Werkzeuge und Technologien effektiv nutzen und in ihren Arbeitsalltag integrieren. Diese Herausforderung stellt viele Organisationen vor komplexe Aufgaben – und genau hier setzt SAP Preferred Success an.

Wie genau unterstützt SAP Preferred Success Unternehmen bei ihrer digitalen Transformation?

SAP Preferred Success ist weit mehr als nur ein Supportmodell – es ist ein umfassender Begleiter für Unternehmen auf ihrer Cloud-Reise mit SAP-Lösungen. Der Service bietet nicht nur Unterstützung bei der initialen Implementierung, sondern steigert langfristig die Produktivität und Effizienz von Organisationen in der Cloud.

Im Mittelpunkt steht ein dedizierter Customer Success Manager, der in regelmäßigen Abstimmungsmeetings einen maßgeschneiderten Erfolgsplan erstellt und dessen Umsetzung begleitet.

Dieser Plan bedient sich aus einem großen Katalog verschiedener Services, die auf die spezifischen Bedürfnisse des jeweiligen Unternehmens zugeschnitten werden. Dazu gehören Solution Launch Checks und Success Checks, die eine optimale Konfiguration der SAP-Lösungen sicherstellen und Verbesserungspotenziale aufdecken.

Ein Highlight ist die detaillierte Release-Begleitung, die tiefe Einblicke in neue Software-Versionen und deren Auswirkungen bietet. Dies ermöglicht eine proaktive Planung und erleichtert die Anpassung an neue Funktionalitäten.

Zur Förderung der kontinuierlichen Weiterbildung beinhaltet das Programm fünf Lizenzen für den SAP Learning Hub. Diese ermöglichen den Zugang zu gezielten Schulungsprogrammen, die auf die spezifischen Anforderungen des Unternehmens abgestimmt sind.

SAP Preferred Success bietet zudem erweiterten Support mit verbesserten Service Level Agreements. Dies gewährleistet schnellere Reaktionszeiten und effizientere Problemlösungen. Bei komplexen Herausforderungen stehen dem Unternehmen Fachexperten mit fundiertem Know-how zur Seite.

Insgesamt zielt SAP Preferred Success darauf ab, Unternehmen die notwendigen Werkzeuge, das erforderliche Wissen und die benötigte Unterstützung zu bieten, um ihre digitale Transformation erfolgreich zu gestalten und den maximalen Nutzen aus ihren SAP-Lösungen zu ziehen.

Eine unabhängige Studie von Forrester Consulting hat die Vorteile von SAP Preferred Success untersucht und quantifiziert. Die Ergebnisse zeigen, dass Unternehmen, die das Programm nutzen, erhebliche Verbesserungen erzielen konnten. Dazu gehören eine gesteigerte Produktivität, reduzierte Systemausfallzeiten und beschleunigte Einführung und Updates. Die Erkenntnisse aus der Studie unterstreichen den Wertbeitrag von SAP Preferred Success für Unternehmen, die ihre SAP-Investitionen optimal nutzen möchten.

Kontinuierliche Weiterentwicklung: Neuerungen bei SAP Preferred Success

SAP Preferred Success entwickelt sich stetig weiter, um mit den sich wandelnden Anforderungen der Kunden und den Fortschritten in der SAP-Technologie Schritt zu halten. In den vergangenen Jahren wurde das Programm nicht nur für eine breitere Palette von SAP-Lösungen ausgerollt, sondern auch um innovative Services erweitert.

Eine Übersicht der SAP-Lösungen, für die SAP Preferred Success verfügbar ist, einschließlich detaillierter Informationen zu jeder Lösung, findet sich hier.

SAP hat mit der Einführung der "Expanded Edition" von Preferred Success einen bedeutenden Schritt nach vorn gemacht. Diese erweiterte Version richtet sich an Unternehmen, die vor komplexeren Herausforderungen stehen oder eine intensivere Betreuung bei der Nutzung ihrer SAP-Lösungen wünschen.

Zusammengefasst bieten die Expanded Editions von SAP Preferred Success eine Reihe von Vorteilen:

  • Tiefgreifenderes Expertenwissen durch einen erweiterten Zugang zu Fachleuten, die ihr umfangreiches SAP Know-How einbringen.
  • Zusätzliche Unterstützung: Neben dem Customer Success Manager steht ein Produktspezialist zur Verfügung, der detaillierte Einblicke bietet.
  • Verbesserte Supportleistungen, um noch schneller und effektiver auf Kundenanfragen reagieren zu können.

Auch zu den Expanded Editions von SAP Preferred Success gibt es eine unabhängige Forrester-Studie, die deren Vorteile für Unternehmen analysiert und bewertet hat. Laut dieser Studie erzielten Unternehmen eine signifikante Effizienzsteigerung. Dies äußerte sich vor allem in einem schnelleren Zugang zu SAP-Expertise, einer deutlichen Reduzierung externer Partnerkosten sowie erheblichen Zeitersparnissen bei der Verwaltung von Anpassungen. Darüber hinaus stellte die Studie eine Verbesserung des internen Fachwissens, eine Beschleunigung von Innovationsprozessen und eine Minderung von Systemrisiken fest.

Neben der Einführung von SAP Preferred Success für zusätzliche SAP-Lösungen und der Erweiterung durch die Expanded Edition für einige der Lösungen, hat SAP Preferred Success auch sein Serviceportfolio erweitert, um den sich wandelnden Bedürfnissen der Kunden gerecht zu werden. Dabei ist zu beachten, dass sich die angebotenen Services je nach SAP-Lösung unterscheiden, um eine möglichst passgenaue Unterstützung zu gewährleisten.

Ein besonderer Schwerpunkt liegt auf den neu eingeführten AI Accelerator Services. Diese innovativen Dienste sind darauf ausgerichtet, Unternehmen bei der Nutzung künstlicher Intelligenz in ihren SAP-Lösungen zu unterstützen und sie auf dem Weg zu einer KI-gestützen, zukunftsorientierten Unternehmensführung zu begleiten. Obwohl die spezifischen Funktionen je nach SAP-Produkt variieren, verfolgen die AI Accelerator Services generell drei Hauptziele:

  1. Innovation: Unterstützung bei der Entwicklung und Implementierung neuer, KI-gestützter Geschäftsmodelle und Prozesse.
  2. Automatisierung: Hilfe bei der Identifizierung und Umsetzung von Automatisierungsmöglichkeiten durch KI, um Effizienz und Produktivität zu steigern.
  3. Prozessoptimierung: Beratung zur Verbesserung bestehender Geschäftsabläufe durch den Einsatz von KI-Technologien.

Beispielsweise könnten die AI Accelerator Services für SAP S/4HANA Kunden dabei unterstützen, intelligente Vorhersagemodelle für die Bestandsoptimierung zu implementieren. Für SAP SuccessFactors-Nutzer könnten sie Hilfestellung bei der Einführung KI-gestützter Rekrutierungsprozesse bieten.

Eine weitere Sparte neuer SAP Preferred Success Services kommt aus dem Bereich Organizational Change Management (OCM). Um dieses Thema abzudecken, das für die Digital Adoption aus menschlicher Sicht unverzichtbar ist, hat das Digital Change and Adoption Team der SAP zwei Services ausgearbeitet:

  • den Transformation Mindset Insights Service sowie
  • den Operational Change & Adoption Readiness Service.

Beide Services nähern sich dem Thema Transformation aus der menschlichen Perspektive, denn der Fakt bleibt: Ohne die Mitarbeiter*innen kann keine Transformation erfolgreich werden.

Der Transformation Mindset Insights Service von SAP hilft Organisationen, ihren Reifegrad in Bezug auf Transformationsfähigkeit zu verstehen und kontinuierlich zu verbessern.

Der Prozess umfasst:

  • Analyse der sechs Mindset Traits, namentlich Resilienz, Systemisches Denken, Kontinuierliche Entwicklung, Cross-Kollaboration, Offenheit für Veränderung und Situative Adaptivität.
  • Datenbasierter Ansatz mit Management-Sicht und Mitarbeiterbefragungen.
  • Workshops zur Erarbeitung einer Roadmap zur Verbesserung des Transformation Mindset Reifegrads.
  • Umsetzung von Maßnahmen in einer mit dem Kunden vereinbarten Aktionsphase.
  • Erneute Erhebung des Reifegrads zur Überprüfung der Effektivität der Maßnahmen.
  • Kontinuierliche Arbeit am Transformation Mindset

So können Organisationen kontinuierlich an ihrem Transformation Mindset arbeiten und das Expertenwissen der SAP bei Bedarf zur Unterstützung heranziehen, um so den neuen Herausforderungen unserer stetig wandelnden Welt einen Schritt vorauszubleiben.

Ein zufriedener Kunde hatte Folgendes zu den Ergebnissen des Transformation Mindset Insights Service zu sagen: „[Der Service] zeigt genau, was wir im letzten Jahr vermutet hatten. Er fängt die Gefühle ein, die mir die Leute hinter verschlossenen Türen mitgeteilt haben. Diese kann ich dem Management nun anhand realer Daten zeigen. Die Ergebnisse spiegeln unsere Situation sehr gut wider.“

Der Operational Change & Adoption Readiness Service richtet sich derweil hauptsächlich an Kunden der Human Capital Management (HCM) Sparte der SAP Lösungen, welche den Reifegrad ihrer OCM Aktivitäten verstehen und verbessern möchten, bevor sie ihre Transformation beginnen.
SAPs OCM Expert*innen evaluieren in einem datenbasierten Vorgehen bestehende OCM Aktivitäten und Konzepte der Kunden und erarbeiten basierend auf den Ergebnissen gemeinsam mit den Kunden Verbesserungsempfehlungen für ihre OCM- und Trainingsplanung. So können Organisationen auch ohne ein ganzes OCM Engagement sicherstellen, dass sie einen gut informierten und effektiven OCM Ansatz für ihre Transformation verfolgen.

Kunden können sich für detaillierte Informationen zu den verfügbaren Services für ihre spezifischen SAP-Lösungen an ihren Customer Success Manager wenden.

Fazit:

SAP Preferred Success entwickelt sich stetig weiter, um den Herausforderungen der digitalen Transformation gerecht zu werden. Mit der Expanded Edition und neuen Services wie den AI Accelerator Services oder Change Management Services unterstreicht SAP sein Ziel, Kunden bei der optimalen Nutzung ihrer SAP-Lösungen zu unterstützen.

Dabei wird individuell auf Kundenbedürfnisse eingegangen und die Wertschöpfung aus SAP-Investitionen verbessert. So bleiben Unternehmen für zukünftige Anforderungen gut gerüstet.

Informieren Sie sich auf SAP Preferred Success, werfen Sie einen Blick auf die ausgesuchten Informationen unten, wenden Sie sich an Ihre SAP-Ansprechperson oder nehmen Sie mit uns Kontakt auf.

Übrigens: Am 25. Juni 2025 werden die BLS AG und die Barmer Krankenversicherung im Rahmen unseres SAP Learning & Adoption Forums in Walldorf über ihre Erfahrungen mit den Services von SAP Preferred Success berichten. Nutzen Sie die Gelegenheit, Fragen zu stellen und in lockerer Atmosphäre den Mehrwert von SAP Preferred Success für Ihre eigene Transformationsreise zu evaluieren. Die Veranstaltung findet hybrid statt. Wir würden uns sehr freuen, Sie vor Ort oder online begrüssen zu dürfen! Hier geht's zur Anmeldung: Registration

Links auf weitere Blogbreiträge und Artikel zu SAP Preferred Success:

Auf Erfolgskurs mit SAP Preferred Success: Einblicke in den Alltag eines Customer Success Partners

Kundenreferenz mit Lufthansa Technik: “Back to standard” – Wie SAP Preferred Success Lufthansa bei der digitalen Adoption unterstützt

SAP Preferred Success: Transforming Challenges into Opportunities for Companies Worldwide in 2024

Aktuelle Kundenreferenzen in MEE:

SAP Preferred Success for S/4HANA

SAP Preferred Success for HCM

SAP Preferred Success für Digital Supply Chain

entry.title: To infinity and beyond: AI expands shopping experience with a world of possibilities
entry.content:
During Black Friday and Cyber Monday, consumers are offered limitless choices, similar to the variety of toys available for children. At first, every toy appears to be the same – mass-produced and uniform – but as kids interact with them, each one develops according to the children’s imagination and preferences. Comparably, as we prepare for shopping scenarios of 2025, these childhood insights are reflected in how AI systems in proactive customer care evolve based on personalized inputs.

An AI-based approach encourages customized interactions, real-time adaptation, and mitigation of decision uncertainty, as it incorporates predicted answers to issues presented to them. An important element in this shopping dynamic, the coordination between AI tools and custom-made efforts aims to pinpoint a change towards more responsive customer support, especially during major shopping events like Black Friday and Cyber Monday.

Technologies for SAP Holiday Season Readiness Program Expansion

For 2025, the SAP Holiday Season Readiness Program is expanding to support more customers than last year. This expansion is led by a greater integration of Preventive Customer Risk Mitigation (PCRM) and intelligent automation to detect and resolve issues before customer impact. The cross-functional collaboration between Support, Cloud Ops, Engineering, and teams plans to ensure faster action and end-to-end visibility.

The improvements integrated into the program since 2024 include:

  • Joule Conversational AI: Joule now allows end-users and internal teams to query case history, check system health, trigger CURE360 reports, and create proactive cases—all via natural language.
  • Agentic AIs: Specialized AI agents now help with scenario planning, task dispatch, risk identification, mitigation planning, and execution monitoring.
  • Smart Notification & Automation Hub: Real-time alerts and automated task execution for infrastructure and application health.
  • Next-Gen CURE360: Deeper root cause analysis, pattern detection, and smart patrol capabilities.

These new technologies use data analytics to predict and prepare for potential issues during the next holiday season. What-if simulations, which forecast the impact of traffic spikes on infrastructure, and pattern recognition, which identify recurring issues and proactive solutions, are two essential factors for AI-powered predictive and preventive analytics. A real-time dashboard with these functions offers a tailored tool to help teams take proactive steps across all system layers.

Collaborative Efforts

The optimization of e-commerce workflows based on real-time data is one of the industry trends we are adopting to enhance customers’ shopping experience. Agentic AI emerges as a prime mover of these efforts compared to the last peak season. The agentic case resolution and AI framework are intended to resolve matters without human intervention and independently conduct monitoring, task orchestration, and mitigation. Moreover, with the assistance of algorithmic learning, Knowledge Base Articles are created to efficiently address similar issues in the future, for quicker and more accurate support.

In our commitment to upgrade customer support through AI, we've introduced new specialized roles and metrics intended to optimize our processes and measure their effectiveness. Key roles, such as AI-Enabled Support Engineers and Preventive Support Engineers, are projected to apply AI for quick decision-making and risk mitigation, while Knowledge Specialists provide accuracy to our knowledge base. Additionally, metrics like AI Deflection Rate and AI-Assisted Resolution Time will allow us to assess how well AI tools resolve issues independently and improve response times.

With this in mind, we are introducing to handle increased traffic and inquiries during the high shopping period the following additional customer support channels:

  • Conversational AI (Joule): open for internal front office teams for queries, e.g. Mission Critical Center (MCC), Technical Operation Manager and Management team. Supports end-user interactions, triggering backend operations on demand.
  • Real Time Support Channel: Promoting the use of real-time support channel
  • Support community: Open dedicated Support Community to customers in scope
  • Live Situation Room Access: High-impact customers can now observe and interact with the real-time resolution channel.

From Cloud Ops to MCC, different roles are shared among teams involved in the preparation for the shopping events of 2025. With a Suite-First strategy, our expert teams seek to provide technical support on integrated product solutions. CURE360-based proactive actions, as well as scenario planning for Smart Resolve and self-healing mechanisms, are some of the features assembled to heighten capacity and handle more significant volumes in the coming years. Furthermore, AI use to deliver these solutions addresses customer expectations towards an AI-first approach. Much like the metaphor of the large offer of toys for kids, increased AI automation is set to provide a personalized customer experience, one that’s familiar with users’ queries, regardless of their initial cross-industries aspect.

The future for Customer Support

Several key measures are planned to analyze the outcomes of the 2025 peak season and further enhance the capabilities of invisible support. First, incident post-mortems will be conducted to thoroughly investigate the root causes of any incidents that may have occurred during the peak season. This analysis will be crucial in identifying areas where proactive prevention could have been more effective, ultimately strengthening the "invisible" nature of support by learning how to avoid similar disruptions in the future.

Secondly, the CURE360 Accuracy Review will be employed to specifically assess the accuracy of risk detection by the CURE360 platform and the effectiveness of proactive case creation. This review will determine how well potential issues were identified before they impacted customers, a core tenet of invisible support. It will also evaluate whether the proactive measures taken successfully prevented customer-facing issues.

Finally, for any issues that were not resolved proactively, a gap analysis will be performed to pinpoint any detection or action gaps. This is vital for enhancing invisible support, as it will highlight where the preventative systems or processes fell short, allowing for targeted improvements to ensure a more seamless and uninterrupted experience for customers in subsequent peak seasons. The insights gained from these analyses will directly inform refinements to the intelligent automation and proactive frameworks like CURE360 and agentic AIs, further embedding the principles of invisible support into SAP's operational readiness. The goal is to learn from the 2025 experience to make future support even more preemptive and less noticeable to the customer.

With Joule, agentic AIs, PCRM, and deep AI integration across the stack, our 2025 Black Friday and Cyber Monday support readiness is envisioned to be smarter, faster, more predictive, and scalable than ever before. At Sapphire 2025, just as children discover new possibilities with their toys, we’ll showcase how SAP is preparing customers for a world of personalized purchase experience in retail events.

A sincere appreciation to Tarun Luthra, Global Head of Technical Support, Industries & Customer Experience, Winston Zhou, Director of Technical Support, Keith Ho, Customer Support Engagement Director, and Bharath Ramamurthy, Vice President of Strategic Initiatives, Customer Support, SAP experts who have contributed to this article.

entry.title: “Live Preventative Recommendations” Application is now live
entry.content:
Our journey began in April 2024 when we initially launched the new “Preventative KBAs & Notes” card in SAP for Me (original blog) delivering preventative solutions for SAP S/4HANA Cloud systems via an AI-based solution recommendation service. The card has now transitioned into a fully fledged application and has been repositioned to the ‘Overview’ area of the Service & Support dashboard in SAP for Me (Going live April 23rd).
